activities_projects.title: Land Revenue System Evaluation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the impact of British land revenue systems
steps: Describe the Permanent Settlement system
Explain how it affected zamindars and peasants
Evaluate whether the system benefited the British more than Indians
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of revenue policies

activities_projects.title: Peasant Economy Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the economic difficulties faced by peasants
steps: Explain why peasants often fell into debt
Describe the role of moneylenders in rural areas
Write how this affected agricultural production

activities_projects.title: Indigo System Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how indigo cultivation was organised
steps: Explain the system of advance loans given by indigo planters
Describe how peasants were forced to grow indigo
Analyse why indigo farming reduced food crop production
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands economic and social effects of indigo cultivation

activities_projects.title: Indigo Revolt Historical Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Study causes and consequences of the Indigo Revolt
steps: List the major causes of the Indigo Revolt
Explain how peasants organised resistance
Write the impact of the revolt on indigo cultivation

activities_projects.title: Historical Source Criticism
steps: Identify a source from the chapter about indigo farming
Explain what information the source provides
Discuss whether the source might show bias
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about historical evidence

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Colonial Policies
steps: Write features of the Permanent Settlement
Write features of the Ryotwari system
Analyse which system placed more burden on peasants

activities_projects.title: Visual Evidence Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Interpret historical illustrations related to indigo farming
steps: Observe an illustration from the chapter
Describe the activities shown in the picture
Explain what the image reveals about working conditions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops skills in analysing visual historical sources

activities_projects.title: Cause–Effect Analysis of Indigo Farming
activities_projects.objective: Understand relationships between policies and outcomes
steps: Write causes of indigo cultivation in India
List effects on peasants and agriculture
Explain the link between these causes and effects

activities_projects.title: Peasant Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Understand historical events from the viewpoint of peasants
steps: Imagine you are a peasant forced to grow indigo
Write a short diary entry describing your daily problems
Explain why you might join the Indigo Revolt
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages empathy and perspective in history

activities_projects.title: Mini Research on Indigo Trade
activities_projects.objective: Explore the global demand for indigo
steps: Research why indigo dye was valuable in Europe
Explain how indigo was produced in India
Discuss how synthetic dyes later affected indigo cultivation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands global trade connections in colonial history
